response.justification: The candidate brings roughly 17 years of professional software engineering experience and genuine technical breadth across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains, which meets the tenure floor. However, the role demands director-level AI strategy expertise with recognized, demonstrated AI products or platforms — the candidate's only AI-adjacent work is two months in a current role touching MCP tooling and agentic workflows. There is no advanced degree in an AI-related field, no cloud platform strategy experience at enterprise scale, and no track record of advising strategic customers, shaping organizational AI direction, or establishing external thought leadership in AI. The gap between this profile and the Principal/Director-level AI Strategy role is too wide to bridge in an interview.
strengths: 17+ years of professional engineering experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure satisfies the minimum tenure requirement
Current role includes AI-agent infrastructure and MCP framework contributions, indicating a recent and genuine pivot toward agentic tooling
Founder of an open-source geo-data platform and a history of community workshops reflect entrepreneurial instinct and a knowledge-sharing disposition
concerns: AI expertise is shallow and extremely recent (approximately 2 months); no demonstrated AI products, ML systems, or platforms built and shipped at scale
No advanced degree in an AI or cloud-related field, and no evidence of cloud platform strategy work beyond AWS CDK usage on a single team
No indicators of director-level IC scope: no enterprise customer advisory work, no industry-recognized AI thought leadership, and no evidence of driving cross-organizational technical strategy

key_factors.factor: AI depth and demonstrated track record
